findings.summary: Tulsi (Ocimum sanctum) extracts have been studied for hepatoprotective and antioxidant properties, with no clinical reports of altered acetaminophen pharmacokinetics at typical culinary or supplemental tea doses.

Eugenol, a major constituent of tulsi, shows only weak in vitro modulation of certain CYP enzymes, and only at concentrations far exceeding what is achievable through normal dietary or tea consumption.
A review of available pharmacovigilance literature found no documented case reports of a clinically significant interaction between Ocimum sanctum preparations and acetaminophen.
